description: Shared Renovate preset for consumer orgs. Extend from a repo's renovate.json with: { "extends": ["github>jr200-labs/github-action-templates"] }

packageRules.description: Group every non-major bump into a single PR per repo. Schedule deliberately omitted so the wave lands immediately; grouping prevents PR-spam without needing a time window. No automerge — humans review every PR.
Major upgrades always require explicit human review

Widen (don't bump) peer-dependency ranges on @opentelemetry/* so consumers stay free to upgrade without lib churn. The @opentelemetry/api package is a pluggable singleton contract — if a library pins a narrow peer range and a consumer has a different copy resolved alongside, provider.register() touches one copy while trace.getTracer() reads the other, resulting in silent no-op spans. Widening keeps the existing range satisfied while also admitting the new version.
Group all GitHub Action version bumps together
Group all Docker base image bumps together

Don't bump the Python runtime constraint — that's a deliberate stack decision and not something Renovate should touch. Also sidesteps the 'pep440: failed to calculate newValue' warning Renovate hits when trying to bump '>=3.12,<3.14' style ranges. review-by: 2026-05-23

Don't bump @duckdb/duckdb-wasm** on the pre-release `-devN.0` channel. Dev45 (2026-04-13) rewrote the HTTPWasmClient fetch path (commit 0c09da6) and broke module instantiation at load time with 'Body is disturbed or locked' — the Response body is consumed twice. Last known-good is 1.33.1-dev42.0. Recheck once duckdb-wasm cuts stable 1.33.1 (or later) with the HTTP refactor fixed. review-by: 2026-05-23

Don't bump @luma.gl/** or @deck.gl/** — @luma.gl/core is pinned at 9.2.6 with an SSR null-safety patch (patches/@luma.gl__core@9.2.6.patch). Bumping deck.gl to 9.3+ peer-requires @luma.gl/core ~9.3.2, which drops the patch via pnpm hoisting. Bumping @luma.gl/webgl alone causes Turbopack build failures (missing Fence export — only in core@9.3.0+). Keep the whole stack on 9.2.x until the patch is upstreamed or Next/Turbopack no longer trips on it. review-by: 2026-05-23

Disable pep621 manager for pyapi-service-kit — it's a private git-pinned dep handled by our custom regex manager. The pep621 manager can't resolve git+https refs (unsupported/unversioned value) and collides with the regex manager, causing 'Ignoring upgrade collision' and no PR being created. review-by: 2026-05-23

Disable Renovate's built-in helm-values manager. We use a custom regex manager (above) that maps ghcr.io image refs to the github-tags datasource instead, which avoids the per-package access list ghcr.io enforces for private packages. Letting both managers detect the same files would create duplicate, conflicting bump attempts (the helm-values one fails with 'Failed to look up docker package ... no-result' on every run).

customManagers.description: Bump private git-pinned Python deps in pep621 pyproject.toml — e.g. '<pkg>[extras] @ git+https://github.com/<org>/<pkg>.git@v0.7.3'

Bump container image tags in IaC Helm values.yaml files — e.g. 'tag: v0.1.27' under 'repository: ghcr.io/<org>/<service>'. Uses the github-tags datasource (not docker), because ghcr.io images are pushed from corresponding github repos with matching git tags, and the App token has cross-repo contents:read but not always packages:read on each individual private package. The capture extracts the org/repo path from the ghcr.io reference and queries github tags directly, which sidesteps the per-package access list ghcr.io enforces for private packages.

Bump inline `image: ghcr.io/<org>/<repo>:vX.Y.Z` refs in IaC service manifests — Helm values.yaml (k8s pod-spec inside `template:` blocks, ARC gha-runner-scale-set, external charts that don't split repository/tag) AND raw Kustomize manifests (statefulset/deployment/daemonset/cronjob/job/pod). Same rationale as the repository:/tag: manager above: github-tags datasource sidesteps the per-package access list ghcr.io enforces for private packages. The semver bound in currentValue ensures floating tags like `:latest` or `:dind` are ignored.

Bump OCI artifacts pinned via Dockerfile `ARG <FOO>_VERSION=vX.Y.Z` paired with a `ghcr.io/<org>/<repo>:${<FOO>_VERSION}` reference in a later RUN/COPY. Renovate's built-in dockerfile manager only recognises ARGs used in FROM lines, so consumption shapes like `oras pull ghcr.io/whengas/dotagents:${DOTAGENTS_VERSION}` (WG-141) are otherwise invisible. Uses github-tags datasource (not docker) for consistency with the values.yaml managers above and to sidestep the per-package GHCR access list when the package is Private. Two regexes combined via matchStringsStrategy=combination so RE2 doesn't have to span newlines — first captures the version, second the depName.
